---
title: "About"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="masthead">
  <span>Electoral Atlas · Spanish Second Republic · 1931–1936</span>
  <div class="lang-switcher">
    <a href="../en/about.html" class="lang-btn active">EN</a>
    <a href="../es/about.html" class="lang-btn">ES</a>
    <a href="../ca/about.html" class="lang-btn">CA</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="page-header">
  <div class="page-header-flag"></div>
  <div class="page-header-label">The team</div>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">About the Project</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">Who we are and what we are trying to understand</p>
</div>

<section class="content-section">

  <!-- PROJECT DESCRIPTION -->
  <div class="section-label">The research</div>
  <h2 class="section-title">Why map the Second Republic's elections?</h2>
  <p class="section-body">
    The Spanish Second Republic (1931–1939) held three parliamentary elections under universal
    suffrage — including the first election in Spain in which women could vote (1933). These
    elections took place in a highly polarised context and their results have been the subject
    of intense historical and political debate ever since.
  </p>
  <p class="section-body">
    Yet comprehensive, systematic, <em>municipal-level</em> data for all three elections has
    never been assembled in a single, consistent, openly accessible dataset. This project
    fills that gap. By recovering and digitising results from provincial archives, the INE,
    and regional records, we aim to provide a resource for historians, political scientists,
    and the public alike.
  </p>

  <div class="pull-quote">
    <p>"The geography of the Republic's elections encodes the geography of the Civil War
    that followed. To understand one, you must understand the other."</p>
    <cite>Project description, 2024</cite>
  </div>

  <!-- TEAM -->
  <div class="section-label mt-lg">The team</div>
  <h3 class="section-title" style="font-size:1.3rem;">Who we are</h3>
  <div class="team-grid">

    <div class="team-card">
      <div class="team-avatar"><img src="images/tonirodon.jpg" alt="Toni Rodon"></div>
      <div class="team-name">Toni Rodon</div>
      <div class="team-role">PI · VEARLYDEM — Voting in Early Democracies: The Case of the Spanish Second Republic</div>
      <p class="team-bio">
        Associate Professor of Political Science at Universitat Pompeu Fabra (Barcelona),
        ICREA Academia Fellow, and Delegate for Knowledge Transfer in Social Sciences and
        Humanities at UPF. His research focuses on political behaviour, comparative
        politics, and historical political economy.
      </p>
      <div class="team-links">
        <a href="https://www.tonirodon.cat" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🌐 Website</a>
        <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/toni-rodon-6a7299375/" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">💼 LinkedIn</a>
        <a href="https://bsky.app/profile/tonirodon.bsky.social" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🦋 Bluesky</a>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="team-card">
      <div class="team-avatar"><img src="images/pauvall.jpg" alt="Pau-Vall Prat"></div>
      <div class="team-name">Pau-Vall Prat</div>
      <div class="team-role">Researcher</div>
      <p class="team-bio">
        Assistant Professor (Profesor lector) at the Universitat de Barcelona and Teaching
        Fellow at the Universitat Oberta de Catalunya. His research examines democratization,
        elite competition, and historical political economy in Europe.
      </p>
      <div class="team-links">
        <a href="https://sites.google.com/site/pauvallprat/" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🌐 Website</a>
        <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pau-vall-i-prat-82550848/" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">💼 LinkedIn</a>
        <a href="https://bsky.app/profile/pauvallprat.bsky.social" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🦋 Bluesky</a>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="team-card">
      <div class="team-avatar"><img src="images/diegomartin.jpg" alt="Diego Martin-Alvarez"></div>
      <div class="team-name">Diego Martin-Alvarez</div>
      <div class="team-role">Research Assistant</div>
      <p class="team-bio">
        Political scientist and MA graduate from the Carlos III-Juan March Institute.
        Since October 2025, he has worked as research assistant in VEARLYDEM at
        Universitat Pompeu Fabra. His interests include historical political economy,
        educational reforms, and political geography.
      </p>
      <div class="team-links">
        <a href="https://dmartinalv.github.io/" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🌐 Website</a>
        <a href="https://es.linkedin.com/in/diego-mart%C3%ADn-%C3%A1lvarez-293a27254" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">💼 LinkedIn</a>
        <a href="https://bsky.app/profile/diegomartin-916.bsky.social" class="team-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">🦋 Bluesky</a>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <!-- FUNDING -->
  <div class="section-label mt-lg">Funding &amp; acknowledgements</div>
  <div class="info-grid">
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Funded by</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">Spanish Ministry of Science, Innovation and Universities</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Project</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">VEARLYDEM</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Host institution</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">Universitat Pompeu Fabra</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Period</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">2023–2026</div>
    </div>
  </div>

</section>

<footer class="site-footer">
  <p>Electoral Atlas of the Spanish Second Republic &nbsp;·&nbsp; Universitat Pompeu Fabra &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2025</p>
</footer>
```
